The Contingency Fund of India Act, 1950

3. Custody of the Contingency Fund and withdrawals there from.

The Contingency Fundï¿½ of India shall be held on behalf of the President by a Secretary to the Government of India in the Ministry of Finance, and no advancesï¿½ shall be made out of such fund except for the purposes of meetingï¿½ unforeseenï¿½ï¿½ expenditureï¿½ pendingï¿½ï¿½ authorizationï¿½ï¿½ ofï¿½ï¿½ such expenditure by Parliament under appropriations made by law.

The Contingency Fund of India Act, 1950

4. Power to makeï¿½ rules.

[(1)]5*ï¿½ For the purpose of carrying outï¿½ theï¿½ objectsï¿½ ofï¿½ thisï¿½ Act,ï¿½ theï¿½ Centralï¿½ Governmentï¿½ mayï¿½ 6*[,ï¿½ byï¿½ notification inï¿½ the Officialï¿½ Gazette,] makeï¿½ rules 7* regulating allï¿½ matters connectedï¿½ with or ancillary to the custody of, the payment ofï¿½ moneys into and the withdrawal of moneys from, the Contingency Fund ofï¿½ India.6*["(2) Every rule made under this section shall be laid, as soonï¿½ as mayï¿½ be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while itï¿½ is inï¿½ session, forï¿½ a totalï¿½ periodï¿½ ofï¿½ thirtyï¿½ daysï¿½ whichï¿½ mayï¿½ beï¿½ comprised inï¿½ one sessionï¿½ or inï¿½ two or more successive sessions. Andï¿½ if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the sessionï¿½ or theï¿½ successive sessions aforesaid, both Houses agree in making anyï¿½ modification in the rule or both Houses agree that the rule should notï¿½ be made,ï¿½ the ruleï¿½ shall thereafter have effect only in such modifiedï¿½ form orï¿½ be ofï¿½ no effect,ï¿½ as theï¿½ case may be; so, however, that anyï¿½ such modificationï¿½ or annulmentï¿½ shall beï¿½ withoutï¿½ prejudiceï¿½ toï¿½ theï¿½ validity of anything previously done under that rule."]

  1. The Act has been extended to-- Goa, Damanï¿½ and Diuï¿½ by Reg. 12 of 1962, s. 3 and Sch.; Dadra andï¿½ Nagar Haveliï¿½ by Reg.ï¿½ 6 ofï¿½ 1963, s.

  4. The proviso omitted by s. 2, ibid., which was previously inserted by Act 10 of 1972, s. 2 (w.e.f. 9-2-1972).
  5. S. 4, numbered as sub-section (1) thereof by Act 51ï¿½ ofï¿½ 1982, s.2.
